title: Label 309 v1 PoE record
description: The top-level Label 309 Proof-of-Existence record, as decoded from the reassembled canonical-CBOR record body carried under transaction metadata label 309. A CBOR map whose only required key is `v` (the version, the literal unsigned integer 1; any other value is rejected as SCHEMA_INVALID_LITERAL). All payload keys are optional at this structural layer but non-empty when present: `items[]` (inline content claims), `merkle[]` (Merkle list commitments), `supersedes` (prior-record tx hash), `sigs[]` (optional authorship signatures), `crit[]` (criticality markers). Beyond these base keys the record has an OPEN extension namespace: a key is admissible when it matches `^x-.+` (vendor/experimental) or `^[a-z]+-.+` (companion namespace) and contains no control characters (U+0000–U+001F, U+007F–U+009F) — the patterns below exclude control characters anywhere in the key; any other unknown key is rejected (SCHEMA_UNKNOWN_FIELD). Extension values may be any value the canonical encoding profile admits (floats and semantic tags are already rejected at decode as MALFORMED_CBOR). STRUCTURAL only — the following are NOT expressible here and are domain-pass checks: at least one of items/merkle must be present (SCHEMA_EMPTY_RECORD); crit[] entry shape (CRIT_SHAPE_INVALID / EXTENSION_UNSUPPORTED_CRITICAL); registry membership of all algorithm identifiers; and every cross-field invariant of the sub-structures.

v.title: Record version
v.description: The Label 309 record version. MUST be the literal unsigned integer 1 (within the pinned 0 .. 2^32 − 1 exact-integer range); any other value is rejected as SCHEMA_INVALID_LITERAL.
